title: How Markovian Lifting Solves the Rough Volatility Problem
description:
*🚀 Master Quantitative Skills with Quant Guild*
https://quantguild.com

*📈 Interactive Brokers for Algorithmic Trading*
https://www.interactivebrokers.com/mkt/?src=quantguildY&url=%2Fen%2Fwhyib%2Foverview.php

*👾 Join the Quant Guild Discord server here*
https://discord.com/invite/MJ4FU2c6c3
___________________________________________
*🪐 Quant Guild Library*
https://github.com/romanmichaelpaolucci/Quant-Guild-Library/tree/main

**TL;DW Executive Summary**
* Empirically, volatility dynamics are better captured using fractional kernels
* Fractional kernels produce non-Markovian paths increasing simulation cost
* We can maintain good model fidelity approximating dynamics by Markovian lifting improving computational efficiency
* Improvements are profound taking a structure that is inadequate for practical use to one that is computationally tractable as seen in the latest models for fitting 0DTE skews

I hope you enjoyed, and I hope you learned something!

- Roman
___________________________________________
*📖 Chapters:*
00:00 - Motivation for Markovian Lifting
00:53 - Visualizing State Space Expansion
07:41 - Master Quantitative Skills
08:38 - The Non-Markovian Culprit
10:43 - Volterra Kernel Selection
11:23 - Laplace Transform of the Kernel
15:27 - Algebraic Proof by the Semi-Group Property
22:13 - Markovian Lifting for Rough Volatility
25:04 - Managing the Singularity at t=0
26:06 - Solving the Approximate Kernel
29:12 - Application to the Rough Heston Model
35:38 - Benefits of Markovian Lifting
36:46 - Implementation in the Literature
